channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: President Ruto Pushes for Full Digital Records to Fix Education System Irregularities
item.description: President William Ruto has called on the Ministry of Education to fully digitise all education records within the next two months. The move aims to boost accountability, transparency, and proper plann...

item.title: Oshiomhole questions Akpabio's eligibility under revised Senate rules
item.description: Senator Adams Oshiomhole don talk say Senate President Godswill Akpabio no dey qualified to hold the office if we follow the new Senate leadership rules. According to Oshiomhole, the amended rules now...

item.title: Gen Z Canadians Travel to the U.S. Primarily Hindered by Cost and Lack of Paid Leave
item.description: A recent survey reveals that Gen Z Canadians (born 1997–2012) continue to show strong interest in travelling to the United States, with 45 per cent likely to visit next year. Among Canadians planning ...

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship: What Australians Should Know
item.description: An outbreak of the Andes strain of hantavirus aboard a cruise ship in the Atlantic has raised public concern, though health officials stress the risk to the general population remains low. The virus, ...

item.title: Haitian Immigrant Charged with Murder of Florida Store Clerk
item.description: Rolbert Joachin, a 40-year-old man who entered the United States illegally from Haiti, has been indicted on first-degree murder charges for the April 2 killing of Nilufa Easmın, a 51-year-old Banglade...

item.title: Court convicts two men for espionage activities on behalf of China
item.description: A UK court has convicted two men of espionage after they were found guilty of spying on behalf of China. The case has drawn significant attention due to the nature of the intelligence gathered and the...

item.title: UK Supreme Court Rules in Government's Favour on Troubles Legacy Case
item.description: The UK Supreme Court has upheld the government's appeal in a significant case concerning the legacy of Troubles-related crimes in Northern Ireland. The ruling concluded that parts of the 2023 Legacy A...

item.title: Tyla clears up misunderstanding about brief Rihanna interaction at Met Gala
item.description: South African singer Tyla has addressed online speculation surrounding a brief interaction with Rihanna at the 2026 Met Gala in New York. The moment, which quickly went viral on social media, was init...

item.title: Political candidates report widespread abuse and intimidation ahead of May elections
item.description: Candidates from across the political spectrum have reported a significant rise in abuse and harassment during the 2026 local and devolved elections in England, Scotland, and Wales. Reports include onl...

item.title: Poorly Secured AI‑Built Web Apps Leak Sensitive Corporate and Personal Data Online
item.description: A new investigation by cybersecurity firm RedAccess has uncovered thousands of AI‑built web applications, created using tools like Lovable, Replit, Base44, and Netlify, that are hosted publicly on the...

item.title: Chief Mountain Border Crossing to Operate for Summer 2026 Season
item.description: The Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) has announced that the Chief Mountain port of entry will reopen for the 2026 summer season, operating from May 15 until September 30. This international border...

item.title: NiMet Warns 19 States of Possible Floods Amid Early Rains
item.description: The Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet) don yan say 19 states across Naija fit experience flash flood because of heavy early rains wey don start. Di agency explain say di ground don hard well well ...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Warns of Heavy Rains Across 30 Counties
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a warning for heavy rainfall expected to hit Nairobi and at least 30 other counties from Thursday evening through next week. According to the advis...

item.title: Kenya Court Allows Sh39.9 Million Sauti Sacco Compensation to Land Buyers
item.description: In Nairobi, the Court of Appeal has decided not to stop Sauti Savings and Credit Co-operative Society (Sacco) from releasing Sh39.9 million to nine individuals involved in a land dispute. The appellan...

item.title: Study Links Eating Behavior to Effectiveness of Ozempic in Type 2 Diabetes
item.description: A recent study in Japan has found that the effectiveness of GLP-1 receptor agonist drugs, such as Ozempic, in managing type 2 diabetes may depend on why individuals overeat. Researchers followed 92 pe...

item.title: Doctors Clarify Hantavirus Outbreak and Low Risk to Canadians
item.description: Canadian health authorities are monitoring a recent hantavirus outbreak linked to a cruise ship, though officials emphasize that the risk to the public remains low. Two Canadians who were on the affec...

item.title: The Strategic Advantage of Openness in Western Societies
item.description: The article argues that the West's historical and contemporary strength derives less from cultural homogeneity and more from openness to diversity, talent, and innovation. Recent statements by U.S. po...

item.title: Governor Dauda Lawal Receives NASSCO Award for Advancing Social Protection and Inclusive Governance in Zamfara
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State don receive National Social Safety Nets Coordinating Office (NASSCO) Award of Excellence for di way him dey run inclusive governance and social protection program...

item.title: Carlyle’s private credit growth slows as redemption limits and market strain bite
item.description: Carlyle Group’s latest earnings report highlights how difficult it has become to expand its private credit business, even as the firm continues to deploy a record amount of capital across private‑mark...

item.title: Forty Years After Chernobyl: The Lasting Impact on Belarus and Nuclear Safety Concerns
item.description: On April 26, 2026, the world marked 40 years since the catastrophic Chernobyl nuclear disaster. Although the plant is located in Ukraine, Belarus suffered the most severe effects due to prevailing wea...
